summary: Follow-up automation and hygiene items discovered while comparing the two-review crosscheck improvement report against the current workspace.

observations.surface: ops/reports release evidence, release closeout, cohort diagnostics
observations.problem: Release readiness evidence is split across closeout, cohort, freshness, bootstrap, raw registry, and test summary reports, while checked-in state and live diagnostic state are still interpreted by humans rather than a single schema-backed model.
observations.why_it_matters: A conditional release-ready state can be overread as clean release-ready when accepted risks, strict rerun failures, and stale review snapshots are not shown together.
observations.automation_candidate: Add a release evidence dashboard report that joins checked_in_state, live_rerun_state, authoritative_for_release, owner, expiry, and next_action for every release gate.
observations.suggested_followup: Automated on 2026-05-01: added ops/scripts/release_evidence_dashboard.py, ops/schemas/release-evidence-dashboard.schema.json, a Make target, and dashboard tests. The dashboard joins checked-in state, live rerun state, accepted risk, owner, expiry, next action, required evidence, and claim provenance labels.

observations.surface: external review report compiler and review closeout docs
observations.problem: The report correctly warns that reviewed claims, checked-in JSON confirmation, schema validation, fingerprint equivalence, diagnostic workspace checks, and live command reruns can be confused, but there is no machine-readable provenance label per claim.
observations.why_it_matters: Future integrated reviews can accidentally imply that every reviewer reran every command, even when some evidence was schema-only or report-derived.
observations.automation_candidate: Define claim provenance labels such as checked_in_json_confirmed, schema_validated, fingerprint_equivalent_to_checked_in, live_command_rerun_confirmed, diagnostic_workspace_only, review_reported_only, inferred_from_file_inventory, and not_reverified_due_environment.
observations.suggested_followup: Automated on 2026-05-01 for release evidence claims: ops/schemas/release-evidence-dashboard.schema.json now requires a provenance_label on every dashboard claim and reserves labels for checked-in JSON, schema validation, fingerprint equivalence, live command rerun, diagnostic workspace-only evidence, review-only evidence, inventory inference, and environment-limited evidence.

observations.problem: The deselection policy already records reason, policy_ref, release_blocking, and expected_to_pass_after_refresh, but it still lacks owner, expires_at, max_allowed_deselected_count, and count-increase or expiry gate effects.
observations.why_it_matters: The current three self-referential deselections are controlled, but a fourth deselection or an expired exception would not automatically escalate during release closeout.
observations.automation_candidate: Extend the deselection policy schema and test execution summary with lifecycle fields, then make release closeout treat expired or increased deselections as attention or fail based on policy.
observations.suggested_followup: Automated on 2026-05-01: test-deselection-policy now requires owner/expiry/budget fields, test-execution-summary writes deselection_lifecycle, reuse refuses stale lifecycle evidence, and release-closeout-summary converts bounded deselections to accepted risk or blockers when lifecycle gates fail.

observations.problem: The report appendix lists multiple release/evidence Make targets as absent, but the current Makefile contains those targets.
observations.why_it_matters: Target inventory drift in a review appendix can misprioritize work and make already-implemented release automation look missing.
observations.automation_candidate: Add a deterministic Makefile target inventory helper used by report compilers instead of hand-built appendix tables.
observations.suggested_followup: Automated on 2026-05-01: added ops/scripts/make_target_inventory.py, ops/schemas/make-target-inventory.schema.json, make-target-inventory Make target, and inventory tests. refresh-generated-observability now refreshes the checked-in inventory.

observations.problem: Bootstrap preflight can pass under the repo-selected .venv while bare python3 fails, but the report does not record environment_class, dependency_source, install_attempted, or interpreter selection intent.
observations.why_it_matters: Without explicit environment classification, review-container failures and release-builder passes can look contradictory rather than intentionally separate evidence classes.
observations.automation_candidate: Add environment_class and interpreter/dependency source fields to bootstrap-preflight reports and document release_builder, developer_cleanroom, review_container, and ci values.
observations.suggested_followup: Automated on 2026-05-01: bootstrap-preflight reports now include an environment block with environment_class, dependency source, install attempt, interpreter, interpreter selection, and include_dev; the Make target exposes BOOTSTRAP_PREFLIGHT_ENVIRONMENT_CLASS.

observations.problem: The checked-in review archive report is schema-backed and valid, but it was generated on 2026-04-28 and does not say whether it represents the current 2026-04-30 post-review workspace.
observations.why_it_matters: A valid archive report can be mistaken for the latest review snapshot when it is only evidence that a prior snapshot was built correctly.
observations.automation_candidate: Add representative_of_current_tree or representative_of_current_zip metadata derived from manifest/input fingerprints and expose stale snapshot status in generated-artifact-index or the release dashboard.
observations.suggested_followup: Automated on 2026-05-01: review_archive report/schema/tests now include manifest_digest, archive_manifest, archive_manifest_digest, and current_snapshot_representativeness, and the review archive report was regenerated from the current workspace.
